activity: Shruti Jain • You AI Systems Engineer | Ex- Founder | Ex- Zoox, Paypal | Swear by Yoga 🧘🏻‍♀️ 22h • Most personas are ineffective. They may look appealing in documents, but they often fail in real-world applications. We have been conducting an experiment to change this approach. Our focus is on creating personas that can be simulated rather than merely described. Instead of relying on assumptions about user behavior, we simulate conversations, flows, and engagement across various personas. The objective is straightforward: transform personas into entities that can be tested. Currently, many teams base product and growth decisions on unvalidated assumptions. By simulating personas, we can anticipate how different users might respond before launching anything. This allows for earlier adjustments and faster iterations. It is becoming increasingly evident that personas should not just be documents; they should be systems that can be tested. At present, we are applying this concept to: → Discord communities → LinkedIn creators → Beta testing If you are developing for users and still depend on static personas, you may be overlooking this crucial shift. Check out the repo and consider giving it a star: https://lnkd.in/gWZdaKqz Feel free to comment or DM me; I am interested in how others are approaching this topic. … more GitHub - agent-persona/personas-pipeline GitHub - agent-persona/personas-pipeline github.com Max Petrusenko and 5 others reacted Max Petrusenko and 5 others 2 comments 2 comments Like Comment Repost Send

Shruti Jain • You AI Systems Engineer | Ex- Founder | Ex- Zoox, Paypal | Swear by Yoga 🧘🏻‍♀️ 3yr • Jeff Hunt • 3rd+ AI Inventor, Solutions Developer, Entrepreneur | Founder of IronRodAI.com & Truthlocked.ai | Co-Founder of MeetElle.ai | Inventor of Patented Truthlock, Elevate Computer Vision Ingest & Elevate GenAI | Salesforce Alumni 3yr • Edited • Disclaimer. This is not legal advice. H1B visa holders who have been impacted by recent layoffs: According to USCIS, people on H1B with approved I-140 MAY be eligible for up to 1 year of compelling circumstances employment authorization (EAD), a temporary stop gap of up to 1 year instead of a 60-day stop gap. There are very few compelling circumstances and this option has a lot of fine print. It’s strongly recommended that anyone thinking of pursuing it consult an immigration attorney to discuss their current circumstances and short/medium/long-term US employment/immigration goals. Not seeking immigration legal counsel can have serious implications. If you see this post please react/repost/comment as it might benefit someone in your network. Options for Nonimmigrant Workers Following Termination of Employment https://lnkd.in/gd8e85m8 #eb2 #GreenCard #I140 #GC #GCBacklog #immigration #ead #employment Check out www.mirajobs.com/layoffs. This is a free database created by volunteers. It’s purpose is to help those who’ve lost their job to match with businesses that are actively hiring. … more Options for Nonimmigrant Workers Following Termination of Employment Options for Nonimmigrant Workers Following Termination of Employment uscis.gov Like Comment Repost Send
